questions.question: What is an undirected graph?
options: A graph where edges go one way
A graph where edges have no direction
questions.answer: A graph where edges have no direction

questions.question: What is a directed acyclic graph (DAG)?

questions.question: What is the time complexity of implementing a queue using two stacks?

options: A tree where every node has exactly 2 children
A tree where all levels are fully filled except possibly the last, filled left to right
A tree with no leaf nodes
questions.answer: A tree where all levels are fully filled except possibly the last, filled left to right

questions.question: What is a perfect binary tree?
options: A tree where all leaf nodes are at the same level and every internal node has 2 children
An AVL tree with balance factor 0
questions.answer: A tree where all leaf nodes are at the same level and every internal node has 2 children

A maximal subgraph where every vertex is reachable from every other vertex

A subgraph with the minimum number of edges
questions.answer: A maximal subgraph where every vertex is reachable from every other vertex

options: Use n & (n-1) in a loop to clear the lowest set bit
Right shift until zero and count

questions.question: What does the 'overlapping subproblems' property mean in DP?
options: The problem can be divided into independent subproblems
The same subproblems are solved multiple times during recursion
The problem has multiple optimal solutions
The subproblems share the same input
questions.answer: The same subproblems are solved multiple times during recursion

questions.question: What is the 'optimal substructure' property in dynamic programming?
options: The problem can be solved in polynomial time
An optimal solution to the problem contains optimal solutions to its subproblems
Subproblems are always smaller by exactly half
The solution can be found greedily
questions.answer: An optimal solution to the problem contains optimal solutions to its subproblems
